titleStmt.title: The Minister in the Dominican Republic ( (Warren) ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Minister in the Dominican Republic ( Warren ) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Ciudad Trujillo , October 20, 1942—11 a.m.
[Received October 22—3:25 p.m.]

div.p: A–136. Department’s instruction No. 158 October 10.Not printed. The Foreign Minister told me today that he had given consideration to the draft agreement proposed by our GovernmentThe draft is almost identical with the signed agreement, printed on p. 276, with the following exceptions: In the signed agreement the amount of money to be provided for the Project is indicated, and in paragraph 4(a) the phrase “from the public domain” is inserted. and that the Dominican Government would be glad to enter into the agreement except that it will be impossible to assume any expenses which might arise under 4 (a) either this year or in 1943. He said that the Government’s financial situation was such that it would be unable to provide raw materials for these projects, and that the road building equipment was so limited that it would not be able to make available any equipment for this work.
Despradel said that if our Government would modify the agreement with respect to these two points the Dominican Government would be glad to accept the text proposed by the Department.
